claim: Code agents (agents writing and executing Python code) outperform tool-calling agents for complex tasks by leveraging the full expressiveness of Python and avoiding rigid tool schemas
actionable: Use smolagents' CodeAgent class instead of ToolCallingAgent when agents need flexibility to write and iterate code (e.g., data analysis, multi-step calculations)

claim: smolagents code agents execute Python in a sandboxed environment (e2b) by default, preventing code injection while allowing libraries like pandas, requests, beautifulsoup via authorized_imports
actionable: Add library names to authorized_imports when instantiating CodeAgent if the agent needs to use packages like requests, pandas, or beautifulsoup; keep sandbox secure by not allowing all imports

claim: Lower-tier local models (Llama 3.2 1B, Qwen 2.5 14B) perform better with structured XML prompts than ad-hoc natural language, showing 5-10x improvement in instruction following
actionable: Structure agent system prompts in XML format with clear sections for instructions, examples, and constraints rather than free-form text to improve local model compliance

claim: Code agents from smolagents can fail silently by exceeding max_iterations without finding a solution; increasing iterations and adding more authorized libraries helps but may hit token limits
actionable: When a code agent hits max iterations without solving the task, check authorized_imports first, increase max_iterations cautiously, and consider breaking into smaller sub-agents or custom tools

claim: smolagents replaces the earlier transformer-agents library and is the recommended Hugging Face agentic framework going forward; transformer-agents is deprecated
actionable: Use smolagents for new projects instead of the older transformer-agents library; migrate existing code if transformer-agents is currently in production

claim: Custom tools in smolagents require strict type hints on input/output; using type hints guides both the model and allows proper marshaling of complex objects between agent steps
actionable: When defining custom Tool subclasses in smolagents, add detailed docstrings and strict type hints on __call__ method parameters and return types for reliable agent behavior

claim: Using LiteLLM with smolagents enables proprietary models (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) in addition to Hugging Face's free-tier models, providing flexibility to switch models based on task complexity
actionable: Import LiteLLMModel from smolagents and pass model_id='gpt-4o' or 'claude-3-opus' to use proprietary APIs alongside local/free-tier models via unified interface

claim: smolagents can push custom tools to Hugging Face Hub, enabling community tool sharing and reuse; other users can import published tools without reimplementing
actionable: When you create a reusable tool, publish it to Hugging Face Hub using smolagents' built-in methods so other developers can discover and import it via smolagents.tools.Hub
